sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.49 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: Does there exist a group$G$satisfying the following four conditions: 1)$G$is simple, moreover, there is an integer$n$such that$G=C^n$for any conjugacy class$C$, 2) all maximal abelian subgroups of$G$are conjugate in$G$, 3) every maximal abelian subgroup of$G$is self-normalizing and it is the centralizer of any of its nontrivial elements, 4) there is an integer$m$such that if$H$is a maximal abelian...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
